closing.summary: Plain Kefir has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.

scenes.narrationText: 3.5g of fat. cholesterol, saturated fat, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for dairy, the fat side can either add richness or drag the whole thing down.

scenes.narrationText: 4.8g of carbs. sugar and glycemic index matter most here. for a dairy food, that is a strong sign.

scenes.narrationText: 3.5g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. useful protein can rescue a lot of weaker traits here.
